title: Multi-Task Head: Next-Lap Pit Plus Laps-To-Stop and Next Compound Family
summary: A next-lap positive is the sharp edge of a broader time-to-stop process.
rationale: A next-lap positive is the sharp edge of a broader time-to-stop process. Recent F1-specific work reports gains from jointly predicting next-lap pit probability and pit horizon; adding next compound family is a natural extra auxiliary target because strategy choice and stop timing are coupled.
implementation_hint: Derive auxiliary train labels such as `laps_until_next_pit` and coarse `next_compound_family` from future rows within each driver-race only for training. Use a shared backbone with classification plus regression/ordinal heads, and score only the binary head at inference.
expected_effect: Should regularize rare-event learning and make the classifier rank imminent stops above merely late-stint laps more consistently.
risk: Auxiliary labels can become sparse or noisy near race end and in wet transitions; weighting the heads poorly can hurt the main AUC.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
